column8: He (to write) books since he was a young man.
column9: What you (to do) here since morning?
column10: Today she (to help) her mother since morning.
column11: They already (to wash) the floor and (to dust) the furniture.
column12: He (to run) for ten minutes without any rest.
column14: I already (to look) for it for two hours, but (not to find) it yet.
column15: I (to wait) for an answer from my cousin for a month already, but (not to receive) it yet.
column16: She already (to do) her homework for two hours; but she (not to do) half of it yet.
column17: It is difficult for me to speak about this opera as I (not to hear) it.
column18: I just (to receive) a letter from my granny, but I (not yet to receive) any letters from my parents.
column19: Jane already (to feed) the cat. You needn’t do it.
